records.ety: [F. ambition, L. ambitio a going around, especially of candidates for office is Rome, to solicit votes (hence, desire for office or honor), fr. ambire to go around. See Ambient, Issue.]

defs: The act of going about to solicit or obtain an office, or any other object of desire; canvassing.
quotes: [I] used no ambition to commend my deeds.

defs: An eager, and sometimes an inordinate, desire for preferment, honor, superiority, power, or the attainment of something.
quotes: Cromwell, I charge thee, fling a way ambition: By that sin fell the angels.
The pitiful ambition of possessing five or six thousand more acres.

defs: To seek after ambitiously or eagerly; to covet.
quotes: Pausanias, ambitioning the sovereignty of Greece, bargains with Xerxes for his daughter in marriage.
